ucs.title: [LLM] Dark Caracal (G0070) Bandook delivery via CHM → cmd/PowerShell → Run-key persistence chain
ucs.description: Hunts the signature Dark Caracal/GDGS infection chain: a trojanized Compiled HTML Help (.chm) file delivered via spearphishing-via-service (Facebook/WhatsApp lures) is opened by hh.exe, which spawns cmd.exe / mshta.exe / powershell.exe to stage the Bandook RAT, followed by HKCU Run-key persistence. This combination (T1218.001 → T1059.003 → T1547.001) is the recurring TTP cluster Lookout and Check Point have repeatedly attributed to Dark Caracal's Bandook campaigns.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=t count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime values(Processes.process) as cmdlines values(Processes.process_path) as paths values(Processes.user) as user from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where Processes.parent_process_name="hh.exe" AND Processes.process_name IN ("cmd.exe","powershell.exe","mshta.exe","wscript.exe","cscript.exe","rundll32.exe") by Processes.dest Processes.parent_process Processes.process_name Processes.process _time span=1h | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)` | join type=outer dest [| tstats summariesonly=t count as run_key_writes values(Registry.registry_path) as run_path values(Registry.registry_value_data) as run_value from datamodel=Endpoint.Registry where Registry.registry_path IN ("*\\Software\\Microsoft\\Windows\\CurrentVersion\\Run\\*","*\\Software\\Microsoft\\Windows\\CurrentVersion\\RunOnce\\*") AND Registry.registry_value_data IN ("*AppData*","*ProgramData*","*Public*","*\\Temp\\*") by Registry.dest | `drop_dm_object_name(Registry)`] | where run_key_writes>0 | eval risk="Dark Caracal Bandook chain: CHM->shell->Run-key" | `security_content_ctime(firstTime)` | `security_content_ctime(lastTime)` | table firstTime lastTime dest user parent_process process_name cmdlines run_path run_value risk
ucs.kql: let chmExec = DeviceProcessEvents | where InitiatingProcessFileName =~ "hh.exe" | where FileName in~ ("cmd.exe","powershell.exe","powershell_ise.exe","mshta.exe","wscript.exe","cscript.exe","rundll32.exe","regsvr32.exe") | extend chmFile = InitiatingProcessCommandLine | project ChmTime=Timestamp, DeviceId, DeviceName, AccountName, ChmCmd=InitiatingProcessCommandLine, ChildProcess=FileName, ChildCmd=ProcessCommandLine; let runKey = DeviceRegistryEvents | where ActionType in ("RegistryValueSet","RegistryKeyCreated") | where RegistryKey has_any (@"\Software\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Run", @"\Software\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\RunOnce") | where RegistryValueData has_any ("AppData","ProgramData",@"\Public\",@"\Temp\",".vbs",".hta",".js",".exe") | project RegTime=Timestamp, DeviceId, RegistryKey, RegistryValueName, RegistryValueData; chmExec | join kind=inner runKey on DeviceId | where RegTime between (ChmTime .. (ChmTime + 30m)) | extend Detection="Dark Caracal G0070 Bandook chain: CHM hh.exe -> shell -> Run key persistence" | project ChmTime, RegTime, DeviceName, AccountName, ChmCmd, ChildProcess, ChildCmd, RegistryKey, RegistryValueName, RegistryValueData, Detection
ucs.rationale: hh.exe spawning a scripting/shell interpreter is rare in benign enterprise telemetry — legitimate help files almost never invoke cmd/powershell/mshta. Dark Caracal's documented Bandook campaigns (Operation Manul, Bandook is Back, Lookout 2018 report) consistently deliver via .chm or trojanized installers from Facebook/WhatsApp lures, drop staged binaries into AppData/Public/Temp, and persist via H
